mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when PAYEMS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4 percent reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and credit costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of unemployment above 7 percent and declining industrial production identifies a contractionary regime where corporate earnings expectations fall sharply, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of UNRATE above 7 percent and falling INDPRO does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In the two years following a business-cycle trough, month-over-month increases in housing starts lead a broad recovery in construction and employment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases in the 24 months following a recession do not produce positive 60-day S&P returns with a hit rate above 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals a sudden drop in manufacturing output and corporate revenues, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO declines exceed 1 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an elevated federal funds rate and falling industrial production indicates the Federal Reserve is tightening into a slowing economy, and this interaction produces sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of FEDFUNDS and INDPRO does not add incremental explanatory power (delta R-squared < 0.02) beyond main effects for 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects the aggregate expansion of domestic economic activity and corporate sales, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly growth does not exhibit a positive rank correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in consumer prices exceeding 0.3 percent forces a rapid upward revision in inflation expectations and discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not negative in at least 55 percent of months when CPIAUCSL accelerates more than 0.3 percent month-over-month.
